foundational_concepts.rule: The basic premise of astrology is that the positions of the Sun, Moon, and some planets can provide important clues about the fortunes of individuals and groups.
Vedic astrology (also called Hindu astrology or Jyotish) is a comprehensive system that can explain differences between individuals born at nearly the same time, like twins.
The four pillars of Vedic astrology are: (1) grahas (planets), (2) rasis (signs), (3) bhavas (houses), and (4) varga chakras (divisional charts).
Vedic astrology uses the Nirayana or sidereal (fixed) zodiac.
The difference between the tropical and sidereal zodiacs is called 'ayanamsa'. The book uses Chitrapaksha/Lahiri ayanamsa.

grahas_planets.rule: A 'graha' or 'planet' is a body that has considerable influence on living beings on Earth.
There are seven primary planets: Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, and Saturn.
Two 'chaayaa grahas' (shadow planets) are considered: Rahu (north node) and Ketu (south node).
There are also 11 moving mathematical points known as Upagrahas (sub-planets).
Lagna (ascendant) is the point rising on the eastern horizon.

rasis_signs.rule: The zodiac is a 360° circle, divided into 12 equal parts of 30° each, called 'rasis' (signs).
The position of a planet in the zodiac is its longitude, measured from the start of the zodiac.
The twelve rasis are: Aries, Taurus, Gemini, Cancer, Leo, Virgo, Libra, Scorpio, Sagittarius, Capricorn, Aquarius, and Pisces.

bhavas_houses.rule: A 'bhava' is a house. Houses are determined with respect to a reference point, most commonly the lagna (ascendant).
The rasi containing the reference point is the 1st house. The next rasi in zodiacal order is the 2nd house, and so on, up to the 12th house.
If no reference point is specified, the lagna is used as the reference for determining houses.
Different houses represent different matters in a person's life.

nakshatras_constellations.rule: Each nakshatra has a length of 13° 20'.
Each nakshatra is divided into 4 quarters called 'padas', with each pada having a length of 3° 20'.

panchaanga_elements.rule: A 'tithi' (lunar day) is the period in which the angular difference between the Moon and Sun changes by exactly 12°.

To calculate the running tithi: Find the difference (Moon's longitude – Sun's longitude), adding 360° if negative. Divide this by 12°. The integer part of the quotient plus 1 gives the index of the running tithi (from 1 to 30).
A lunar month has 30 tithis, divided into two 'pakshas' (fortnights): Sukla/Suddha (brighter, waxing moon) and Krishna/Bahula (darker, waning moon).
A 'yoga' is calculated by adding the longitudes of the Sun and Moon. Divide the sum by 13°20' (800'). The integer part of the quotient plus 1 is the index of the running yoga.
Each tithi is divided into 2 'karanas'. There are 11 karanas in total.
A 'hora' is one of 24 equal parts of a day (from sunrise to the next sunrise). Each hora is ruled by a planet in the order: Saturn, Jupiter, Mars, Sun, Venus, Mercury, Moon.
The first hora of a day is ruled by the lord of that weekday.
Panchaanga refers to the five limbs of the almanac: tithi, vaara (weekday), nakshatra, yoga, and karana.

dasa_systems.rule: Dasa systems divide a person's life into periods and sub-periods ruled by different planets or rasis.
Dasas (main periods) and antardasas (sub-periods) are analyzed based on the natal chart to predict results for different time periods.
Some dasa systems are specialized; for example, Drigdasa for spiritual progress and Sudasa for wealth.

body_part_association.rule: The zodiac represents Lord Vishnu's body, and each rasi corresponds to a specific limb, which can be used to analyze health problems related to that body part.

elemental_classification.indication: A house in a fiery sign can show an angry, aggressive, or determined nature related to that house's matters.
A house in an earthy sign can show a balanced, logical, and stable nature related to that house's matters.
A house in an airy sign can show unstable and wandering emotions or thoughts related to that house's matters.
A house in a watery sign can show an imaginative and creative mind related to that house's matters.

rising_classification.rule: Planets in these rasis give their results in the first half of their dasas.
Planets in these rasis give their results in the second half of their dasas.

rising_classification.classification: Rises with both head and feet

indications: best friend or a worst enemy

general_concepts.rule: There are 7 primary planets (Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn) and 2 shadow planets (Rahu, Ketu).
Rasis represent situations and influences, while planets represent human beings involved in one's life.
Planets are manifestations of Vishnu's avataras (incarnations).

natural_disposition.rule: Mercury is a natural benefic when alone or with other benefics.
The waxing Moon (Sukla paksha) is a natural benefic.
Mercury becomes a natural malefic when joined by more natural malefics.
The waning Moon (Krishna paksha) is a natural malefic.

rules.strength_period: Dakshina ayana (Sun's transit from Cancer to Sagittarius)
Uttara ayana (Sun's transit from Capricorn to Gemini)

planetary_dignities.rule: A planet is strong when placed in its own rasi, exaltation rasi, or moolatrikona rasi.

planetary_dignities.analogy: Own rasi is like one's home (comfortable). Moolatrikona is like one's office (powerful, duty-minded). Exaltation is like a favorite party (excited). Debilitation is like one's worst party (unhappy).

planetary_relationships.rule: For each planet, its relationship with others (friend, neutral, enemy) is fixed. The lord of the planet's exaltation rasi, and lords of the 2nd, 4th, 5th, 8th, 9th, and 12th rasis from its moolatrikona, are its friends. Lords of other rasis are enemies.
Planets occupying the 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 10th, 11th, and 12th rasis from a given planet are its temporary friends in a specific chart. Planets in other rasis are its temporary enemies.
Compound relationships are determined by combining natural and temporary relationships.
A planet is considered to be in a 'friendly house' if the rasi is owned by its mitra or adhimitra (compound relationship).
A planet is considered to be in an 'inimical house' if the rasi is owned by its satru or adhisatru (compound relationship).

introduction_to_upagrahas.rule: There are 11 Upagrahas (sub-planets or satellites) defined by Sage Parasara, which are significant mathematical points rather than physical bodies.

sun_based_upagrahas.rule: The five Sun-based Upagrahas (Dhuma, Vyatipaata, Parivesha, Indrachaapa, and Upaketu) are very malefic in nature.

sun_based_upagrahas.prediction_rule: Any house occupied by a Sun-based Upagraha in the rasi chart or divisional charts is spoiled by it.

other_upagrahas.rule: The nature of the other six upagrahas is similar to that of the main planets.

other_upagrahas.calculation_rule_step1: To calculate these upagrahas, the duration of the day (sunrise to sunset) or night (sunset to sunrise) of birth is divided into 8 equal parts.

other_upagrahas.calculation_rule_step2: Each part is ruled by a planet, determined by the weekday and whether the birth is during the day or night (as per Table 10).

other_upagrahas.calculation_rule_step3: The longitude of an upagraha is the longitude of the ascendant (lagna) at a specific moment within the part ruled by its associated planet.

timing_rules.Kaala: The ascendant's longitude at the middle of the Sun’s part.
timing_rules.Mrityu: The ascendant's longitude at the middle of Mars’s part.
timing_rules.Artha Praharaka: The ascendant's longitude at the middle of Mercury’s part.
timing_rules.Yama Ghantaka: The ascendant's longitude at the middle of Jupiter’s part.
timing_rules.Gulika: The ascendant's longitude at the middle of Saturn’s part.
timing_rules.Maandi: The ascendant's longitude at the beginning of Saturn’s part.

general_principles.rule: Special lagnas are used in addition to the normal ascendant to analyze specific areas of life.
Ghati Lagna (GL) is very sensitive to birth time errors; a one-minute change in birth time can alter GL's position by 1.25 degrees.
The recommended definition of sunrise for these calculations is the time when the upper tip of the Sun's visual disk appears on the eastern horizon.

purpose_of_special_lagnas.represents: Self from the point of view of money, wealth, and prosperity.
Self from the point of view of fame, power, and authority.

calculation_steps: Find the time of sunrise and the Sun's longitude at sunrise.
Calculate the difference between birth time and sunrise time in minutes.
The time difference in minutes equals the advancement of BL in degrees.
Add this advancement to the Sun's longitude at sunrise.
Expunge multiples of 360° to get the final longitude of BL.
calculation_rules.rate_of_movement: 1 degree per 4 minutes (1 rasi per 2 hours).

calculation_steps: Find the time of sunrise and the Sun's longitude at sunrise.
Calculate the difference between birth time and sunrise time in minutes.
Divide the time difference in minutes by 2 to get the advancement of HL in degrees.
Add this advancement to the Sun's longitude at sunrise.
Expunge multiples of 360° to get the final longitude of HL.
calculation_rules.rate_of_movement: 0.5 degrees per minute (1 rasi per hour).

calculation_steps: Find the time of sunrise and the Sun's longitude at sunrise.
Calculate the difference between birth time and sunrise time in minutes.
Multiply the time difference in minutes by 5 and then divide by 4 to get the advancement of GL in degrees.
Add this advancement to the Sun's longitude at sunrise.
Expunge multiples of 360° to get the final longitude of GL.
calculation_rules.rate_of_movement: 1.25 degrees per minute (1 rasi per 24 minutes).

calculation_steps: Find the constellation (nakshatra) occupied by the Moon.
Calculate the fraction of the constellation that the Moon has traversed (distance from start of nakshatra / total length of nakshatra).
Take the same fraction of the entire zodiac (fraction x 360°).
Add this calculated amount in degrees to the longitude of the normal lagna.
Expunge multiples of 360° to get the final longitude of SL.

application_rules.prediction_rule: When timing good and bad periods for a businessman, Hora Lagna (HL) may be very important.
When timing good and bad periods for a politician, Ghati Lagna (GL) may be very important.

general_principles.rule: Divisional charts (Vargas) are created by dividing each rasi into a number of equal parts and mapping each part to a new rasi according to a specific rule.
Each divisional chart signifies a particular area of life and should be analyzed to understand that specific area.
All standard astrological principles (planets, rasis, houses) are applicable to all divisional charts for interpretation, but the context is limited to the chart's specific signification.

general_principles.prediction_rule: The key to correct analysis is to select the divisional chart corresponding to the matter of interest and analyze the relevant houses and planets within that chart.

divisional_chart_significations.area_of_life: Everything related to brothers and sisters.
Residence, houses owned, properties and fortune.
Everything related to children and grand-children.
Sudden and unexpected troubles, litigation etc.
Marriage and everything related to spouse(s), dharma (duty and righteousness), interaction with other people, basic skills, inner self.
Career, activities and achievements in society.
Everything related to parents (also uncles, aunts and grand-parents, i.e. blood-relatives of parents).
Evils and punishment, sub-conscious self, some diseases.
Karma of past life, all matters.

planes_of_operation.rule: These charts show physical matters like body, wealth, residence, wife, children, and parents.
These charts (D-16, D-20, D-24) show matters of the mind and intellect, such as pleasure, unhappiness, religiousness, learning, and knowledge.
These charts (D-27, D-30) relate to the subconscious self, including strengths, weaknesses, inherent nature, and psychological imbalances.
Higher divisional charts (D-40, D-45, D-60) relate to past life karma that influences the current life beyond the physical, mental, and sub-conscious levels.

D-1_Rasi.rule: Each 30° arc is mapped sequentially to the 12 rasis starting from Aries.

D-2_Hora.rule: Each rasi is divided into two 15° parts. For odd rasis, the first part is Sun's hora and the second is Moon's hora. For even rasis, the first is Moon's hora and the second is Sun's hora.

D-3_Drekkana.rule: Each rasi is divided into three 10° parts. The parts are mapped to the 1st, 5th, and 9th rasi from the rasi itself, respectively.

D-4_Chaturthamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into four 7.5° parts. The parts are mapped to the 1st, 4th, 7th, and 10th rasi from the rasi itself, respectively.

D-5_Panchamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into five 6° parts. For odd rasis, parts map to Ar, Aq, Sg, Ge, Li. For even rasis, parts map to Ta, Vi, Pi, Cp, Sc.

D-6_Shashthamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into six 5° parts. For odd rasis, counting starts from Aries. For even rasis, counting starts from Libra.

D-7_Saptamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into seven parts. For odd rasis, counting starts from the rasi itself. For even rasis, counting starts from the 7th rasi from it.

D-8_Ashtamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into eight parts. For movable rasis, counting starts from Aries. For fixed rasis, counting starts from Sagittarius. For dual rasis, counting starts from Leo.

D-9_Navamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into nine 3°20' parts. For fiery rasis (Ar, Le, Sg), counting starts from Aries. For earthy rasis (Ta, Vi, Cp), counting starts from Capricorn. For airy rasis (Ge, Li, Aq), counting starts from Libra. For watery rasis (Cn, Sc, Pi), counting starts from Cancer.

D-10_Dasamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into ten 3° parts. For odd rasis, counting starts from the rasi itself. For even rasis, counting starts from the 9th rasi from it.

D-11_Rudramsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into eleven parts. Counting starts from the rasi found by counting anti-zodiacally from Aries the same number of places as the rasi in question is from Aries zodiacally.

D-12_Dwadasamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into twelve 2°30' parts. Counting for all parts starts from the rasi itself.

D-16_Shodasamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into sixteen parts. For movable rasis, counting starts from Aries. For fixed rasis, counting starts from Leo. For dual rasis, counting starts from Sagittarius.

D-20_Vimsamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into twenty parts. For movable rasis, counting starts from Aries. For fixed rasis, counting starts from Sagittarius. For dual rasis, counting starts from Leo.

D-24_Chaturvimsamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into twenty-four parts. For odd rasis, counting starts from Leo. For even rasis, counting starts from Cancer.

D-27_Nakshatramsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into twenty-seven parts. For fiery rasis, counting starts from Aries. For earthy rasis, from Cancer. For airy rasis, from Libra. For watery rasis, from Capricorn.

D-40_Khavedamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into forty parts. For odd rasis, counting starts from Aries. For even rasis, counting starts from Libra.

D-45_Akshavedamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into forty-five parts. For movable rasis, counting starts from Aries. For fixed rasis, from Leo. For dual rasis, from Sagittarius.

D-60_Shashtyamsa.rule: Each rasi is divided into sixty 30' parts. Counting starts from the rasi itself. To find the part number, multiply the degrees within the rasi by 2, take the integer part, and add 1.

general_rule.rule: A planet's strength (Amsabala) is judged by counting how many times it falls in its moolatrikona, own rasi, or exaltation rasi within a specific group of divisional charts. A higher count indicates a stronger planet.

dasa_varga.description: A group of 10 divisions: D-1, D-2, D-3, D-7, D-9, D-10, D-12, D-16, D-30, and D-60.

prediction_rules.rule: A Lagna lord or Ghati Lagna lord in Simhaasanaamsa (or higher) can make a person very famous.
A quadrant lord with good amsabala in dasa varga makes one very successful.

general_principles.rule: A house is a rasi. To determine houses, a point of reference is chosen. The rasi containing the reference point is the 1st house, the next rasi in zodiacal order is the 2nd house, and so on up to the 12th.
If no reference point is specified, the Lagna (ascendant) is the default reference for determining houses.

general_principles.interpretation_rule: The matters signified by a house are colored by two factors: (1) the nature of the reference point used, and (2) the specific area of life represented by the divisional chart being analyzed.

general_principles.methodology_rule: The recommended method is to treat each rasi as one house ('Rasi = Bhava'). Systems that create a 'bhaava chakra' where houses span across rasis are not recommended by the author.

general_principles.interpretation_rule: The meanings of houses can be derived by counting from other houses. For example, the 4th house, being the 2nd from the 3rd house, can show the wealth (2nd house matter) of a younger sibling (3rd house matter).

reference_points_for_houses.interpretation: Represents the 'true self', 'conceptual self', and the 'spirit of I'. Used to analyze intentions, knowledge, and persistence.
Represents the 'mind'. Houses counted from the Moon show matters from the perspective of the mind, such as happiness, ambition, and one's mental view of their career.
Represents the 'soul' and 'physical vitality'. Houses counted from the Sun show matters from the soul's perspective.
Represents how a native is 'perceived in the world' and their 'status'. Matters related to public image and social standing are judged from here.
Represents the 'physical self' or 'physical existence' of the native. Houses from Paaka Lagna show matters related to the physical self, such as physical vitality or memory.

reference_points_for_houses.reference: Karakamsa Lagna (Position of Atma Karaka in D-9)
reference_points_for_houses.interpretation: Represents the 'soul' and is an important reference in the Navamsa (D-9) chart for analyzing the 'inner self'.
Represents 'self from the point of view of power, authority, and fame'. Crucial for analyzing career promotions and political power.
Represents 'self from the point of view of wealth'. Important for financial analysis.

reference_points_for_houses.interpretation_rule: To analyze matters of a specific house, houses can be counted from the natural significator (karaka) of that house or matter. The analysis from the graha lagna should be synthesized with the analysis from the primary lagna. If the karaka planet is stronger than the lagna, the houses from the karaka may be more important.

special_house_categories_and_their_meani.prediction_rule: Trines are the abode of Goddess Lakshmi and represent prosperity. Houses in trine to a reference point are beneficial to it and bring prosperity and well-being.
Quadrants are the abode of Lord Vishnu and represent sustenance and vital activity. They sustain the matters of the reference point.
Upachayas from a reference point show forces that cause gains and growth for the matters signified by that reference.

Dusthanas from a reference point show forces that cause setbacks and obstacles to its matters. A fortified dusthana indicates serious obstacles, while a weak one shows obstacles will be easily overcome.

sub_categories.name: Dharma Trikona (Trines of Duty)
sub_categories.purpose: Represents righteousness and adherence to one's duty.

sub_categories.name: Moksha Trikona (Trines of Liberation)
sub_categories.purpose: Represents the path to final liberation of the soul.

halves.name: Visible Half
halves.interpretation: Results manifest in the material world and can be seen easily.

halves.name: Invisible Half
halves.interpretation: Results are internal and cannot be easily seen in the material world.

introduction_to_karakas.rule: A 'karaka' is a significator, the one who causes events related to a specific matter.

introduction_to_karakas.purpose: Represent everything in creation (people, things, matters). Used for general predictive astrology (Phalita Jyotish).
Represent people who play a significant role in one's life regarding sustenance, achievements, and spiritual progress. Used for Raja Yogas and spiritual analysis.
Represent the destruction of the body. Used specifically for timing the death of near relatives.

introduction_to_karakas.application_rule: The three types of karakas should not be used interchangeably as each has a specific purpose.

chara_karakas_variable.purpose: To analyze the inner self and the people influencing one's sustenance, achievements, and spiritual evolution.

calculation_rule.description: Take the 8 planets: Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, and Rahu.
Find the longitude of each planet within its rasi (0° to 30°). For Rahu, measure its advancement from the end of the rasi (30° minus Rahu's longitude in the sign).
Arrange these 8 planets in descending order based on their advancement.
Assign karaka status based on the rank. The highest is Atma Karaka (AK), the lowest is Dara Karaka (DK).

application_rule.rule: The Chara Karaka planet itself represents the person in question (e.g., DK represents the spouse). Houses are not counted from it for this purpose.

sthira_karakas_fixed.purpose: Used exclusively for timing the death of relatives.

significators.karaka: The stronger of Sun or Venus
The stronger of Moon or Mars

significators.relative: Husband, sons, paternal grandparents, paternal relatives

application_rules.rule: Sthira Karakas must not be used for general predictions; their use is restricted to longevity analysis of relatives.
The Sthira Karaka planet itself represents the physical body of the relative for timing death (e.g., Jupiter represents the husband's physical body in a female chart for this purpose).

naisargika_karakas_natural.purpose: Used for general predictions in all areas of life (Phalita Jyotish).

application_rule.rule: To analyze a matter, the house corresponding to that matter must be analyzed from the Naisargika Karaka planet as the reference point (e.g., to analyze mother, check the 4th house from the Moon).

foundational_concepts.rule: Lagna represents the 'true self', while Arudha Lagna (AL) represents how the native is 'perceived in the material world' (the maya or illusion associated with the self). Both are essential for a complete astrological analysis.

For predicting materialistic matters like career success, status, and public image, perceptions are often more important than the underlying reality. Arudha padas are the primary tool for analyzing these perceptions.

bhava_arudhas.rule_name: Calculation of Arudha Pada of a House (Bhava)
steps: Identify the rasi containing the house in question.
Find the rasi occupied by the lord of that house. For signs with co-lords (Scorpio and Aquarius), use the stronger lord.
Count the number of rasis zodiacally from the house's rasi to its lord's rasi.
Count the same number of rasis from the lord's rasi to arrive at the provisional arudha pada.

Apply the exception: If the resulting rasi from step 4 is the 1st or 7th from the original house's rasi (from step 1), then the final arudha pada is the 10th rasi from the provisional one. Otherwise, the provisional arudha pada is the final one.

graha_arudhas.rule_name: Calculation of Arudha Pada of a Planet (Graha)
steps: Identify the rasi containing the planet in question.
Find the stronger of the two signs owned by that planet (for planets owning two signs).
Count the number of rasis zodiacally from the planet's rasi to its stronger owned rasi.
Count the same number of rasis from the stronger owned rasi to arrive at the provisional graha arudha.

Apply the exception: If the resulting rasi from step 4 is the 1st or 7th from the planet's original rasi (from step 1), then the final graha arudha is the 10th rasi from the provisional one. Otherwise, the provisional graha arudha is the final one.

three_layered_analysis_framework.rule: A complete analysis requires judging events from three perspectives:

perspectives.analysis_of: Positions of planets with respect to Lagna
Positions of planets with respect to Arudha Lagna (AL)
perspectives.reveals: The perception of the world, the material situation, and one's status.

perspectives.analysis_of: Positions of the Arudha Padas of planets (Graha Arudhas) with respect to Lagna
perspectives.reveals: The native's own perception of the situation and how the native feels.

arudha_lagna_al_rules.rule: The 11th and 12th houses from AL indicate financial gains and expenditures, respectively.
Natural malefics in the 3rd and 6th houses from AL create the perception of a bold, successful person who defeats enemies, leading to material success.
Natural benefics in the 3rd and 6th houses from AL create the perception of a gentle, mild-natured, and restrained person, often found in the charts of saints.
The 10th house from AL in the D-10 chart relates to one's status in their career, as opposed to the 10th from Lagna, which shows the true nature of their work.

bhava_arudha_rules.rule: The arudha pada of a house (An) shows the tangible 'maya' or illusion associated with that house's matters. It signifies the physical manifestations that create the world's perception.

prediction_rules: Planets in UL indicate the nature of the spouse.
The 8th house from UL shows the longevity of the marriage.
The 2nd and 7th houses from UL show the end of the marriage. Malefics in these houses can cause troubles or divorce.
In D-16 (vehicles/comforts), A4 represents the physical vehicle owned by the person, which shapes the world's perception of their comfort.
In D-24 (education), A4 represents the school, college, or university where one studies, which shapes the perception of their learning.
In D-10 (career), A10 represents the workplace environment, which shapes the perception of one's career.

bhava_arudha_rules.prediction_rule: A7 shows the kind of people one associates with, which shapes the world's perception of one's relationships.
A3 represents the maya of one's communication or boldness. In D-10 or D-24, it can represent the books or articles one has written.

prediction_rules: In D-20 (religion), A5 represents the mantras, rituals, and poojas one performs.
In D-10 (career), A5 represents the trappings of power and awards one receives.
In D-24 (education), A5 represents one's performance in examinations and academic distinctions.

bhava_arudha_rules.prediction_rule: In D-24 (education), A9 can show the higher degrees one has received.

graha_arudha_rules.rule: The position of a planet's arudha pada from the Lagna reveals how the native will perceive events or feel during that planet's dasa (period), which may differ from both the reality (planet from Lagna) and the world's perception (planet from AL).

graha_drishti_planetary_aspects.rule: All planets cast a full aspect on the 7th house/rasi from their position.

graha_drishti_planetary_aspects.special_aspects: In addition to the 7th, Jupiter aspects the 5th and 9th houses/rasis from its position.
In addition to the 7th, Mars aspects the 4th and 8th houses/rasis from its position.
In addition to the 7th, Saturn aspects the 3rd and 10th houses/rasis from its position.

graha_drishti_planetary_aspects.application_rule: When a planet aspects a house, it also aspects any planets located within that house.

rasi_drishti_sign_aspects.rule: A movable rasi aspects all fixed rasis (Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, Aquarius) except the one adjacent to it.
A fixed rasi aspects all movable rasis (Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn) except the one adjacent to it.
A dual rasi aspects all other dual rasis.

rasi_drishti_sign_aspects.application_rule: A planet casts a rasi drishti on all the rasis that are aspected by the rasi it occupies. This aspect affects the houses and any planets within those aspected rasis.

argala_intervention.definition: Argala means 'bolt' and represents a decisive influence or intervention on the affairs of a house or planet.

argala_intervention.rule: Planets or houses located in the 2nd, 4th, and 11th houses from a reference house/planet cause primary argala on it.
A planet or house in the 5th house from a reference house/planet causes a secondary argala on it.

classification.name: Subhaargala
classification.description: A benefic intervention caused by a natural benefic planet.

classification.name: Paapaargala
classification.description: A malefic intervention caused by a natural malefic planet.

virodhargala_obstruction_to_intervention.definition: Virodhargala represents the obstruction of an argala.

virodhargala_obstruction_to_intervention.rule: The obstruction to an argala is caused by planets or houses located at specific positions from the reference point:

virodhargala_obstruction_to_intervention.application_rule: To determine the net effect, one must assess if the argala or the virodhargala is stronger, based on the number and strength of the planets involved.

special_argala_rules.rule: Argala and Virodhargala on that rasi (and on Ketu) must be counted in the anti-zodiacal (reverse) direction.

special_argala_rules.condition: If there are several malefics in the 3rd house from a reference house/planet
special_argala_rules.rule: They cause argala instead of virodhargala (obstruction) on that reference point.

argala_interpretation_rules.interpretation: Represents the basic ingredient for the sustenance of the matter (e.g., food for the body).
Represents the basic factor that drives the mood, state, and progress of the matter (e.g., comfort for the self).
Represents the catalyst that can result in gains for the matter.
Represents additional contributing factors (e.g., emotions for the self, hard work for learning).

introduction.rule: Yogas are specific planetary combinations that give specific results.

introduction.application_rule: Yogas are applicable in divisional charts, but their results should be interpreted based on the matters signified by that specific divisional chart.

ravi_yogas_solar.definition: A planet, other than the Moon, is in the 2nd house from the Sun.
ravi_yogas_solar.results: The person has a balanced outlook, is truthful, tall, sluggish, happy, and comfortable even with little wealth.

ravi_yogas_solar.definition: A planet, other than the Moon, is in the 12th house from the Sun.
ravi_yogas_solar.results: The person is skillful, charitable, famous, learned, and strong.

ravi_yogas_solar.definition: Planets, other than the Moon, are in both the 2nd and 12th houses from the Sun.
ravi_yogas_solar.results: The person has all comforts and is a king or equal to a king.

ravi_yogas_solar.definition: The Sun and Mercury are in the same sign (conjunction).
ravi_yogas_solar.results: The person is intelligent, skillful in all works, well-known, respected, and happy.
ravi_yogas_solar.note: The yoga's power is reduced if Mercury is combust (too close to the Sun). It is most powerful in divisional charts like D-10.

general_guidelines.rule: If Moon is in a quadrant from Sun: little wealth, intelligence, and skills. If in a panapara from Sun: average wealth, intelligence, and skills. If in an apoklima from Sun: a lot of wealth, intelligence, and skills.

If Moon is in its own navamsa or that of a good friend (adhimitra): For a daytime birth, Jupiter's aspect on Moon brings wealth and comforts. For a nighttime birth, Venus's aspect on Moon brings the same. The reverse aspects are detrimental.
If all natural benefics are in upachayas (3, 6, 10, 11) from Moon: great wealth. If two benefics: medium wealth. If one benefic: little wealth.

specific_yogas.definition: Planets, other than the Sun, are in the 2nd house from the Moon.
specific_yogas.results: The person becomes a king or equal, is intelligent, wealthy, famous, and has self-earned wealth.

specific_yogas.definition: Planets, other than the Sun, are in the 12th house from the Moon.
specific_yogas.results: The person becomes a king with good looks, is free from disease, has good character, great reputation, and is surrounded by comforts.

specific_yogas.definition: Planets, other than the Sun, are in both the 2nd and 12th houses from the Moon.
specific_yogas.results: The person enjoys many pleasures, is charitable, and owns wealth, vehicles, and good servants.

specific_yogas.definition: There are no planets (other than the Sun) in the 2nd and 12th houses from the Moon, AND there are no planets in the quadrants from the lagna.
specific_yogas.results: This is a bad yoga. The person is unlucky, lacks intelligence and learning, and is afflicted by poverty and trouble. It can nullify other good yogas.

specific_yogas.definition: The Moon and Mars are in the same sign (conjunction).
specific_yogas.results: The person is worldly-wise and materially successful, may earn through unscrupulous means, and may treat mother or other women badly.

specific_yogas.definition: Natural benefics (Mercury, Jupiter, Venus) occupy the 6th, 7th, and 8th houses from the Moon.
specific_yogas.results: The person becomes a king, a minister, or an army chief, depending on the strength of the planets involved.

pancha_mahapurusha_yogas.definition: Mars is in a quadrant (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th) from the lagna and is in its own sign (Aries, Scorpio) or exaltation sign (Capricorn). Applies mainly in the rasi chart.
pancha_mahapurusha_yogas.results: A great person of fiery nature. Enthusiastic, a natural leader, victorious in fights, discriminative, devotee of the learned, well-versed in occult sciences, successful.

pancha_mahapurusha_yogas.definition: Mercury is in a quadrant from the lagna and is in its own sign (Gemini, Virgo) or exaltation sign (Virgo). Applies mainly in the rasi chart.
pancha_mahapurusha_yogas.results: A great person of earthy nature. Lion-like, learned, good physique, deep voice, sattwa guna, knows yoga, systematic, clean, independent, and religious.

pancha_mahapurusha_yogas.definition: Saturn is in a quadrant from the lagna and is in its own sign (Capricorn, Aquarius) or exaltation sign (Libra). Applies mainly in the rasi chart.
pancha_mahapurusha_yogas.results: A great person of airy nature. Rabbit-like, wise, enjoys wandering, comfortable in forests/mountains, valorous, slender build, knows weaknesses of others, lively, charitable.

pancha_mahapurusha_yogas.definition: Venus is in a quadrant from the lagna and is in its own sign (Taurus, Libra) or exaltation sign (Pisces). Applies mainly in the rasi chart.
pancha_mahapurusha_yogas.results: A great person of watery nature. Lustrous, enjoys tasty food, has luxuries, excellent health, well-versed in arts.

pancha_mahapurusha_yogas.definition: Jupiter is in a quadrant from the lagna and is in its own sign (Sagittarius, Pisces) or exaltation sign (Cancer). Applies mainly in the rasi chart.
pancha_mahapurusha_yogas.results: A great person of ethery nature. Swan-like, has spiritual strength and purity, respected by all, passionate, becomes a king, has all comforts, clever conversationalist.

basics.definition: The lord of a quadrant (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th) and the lord of a trine (1st, 5th, 9th) are associated.
basics.results: The native becomes powerful and prosperous.

basics.definition: A special case of Raaja Yoga where the lords of the 9th house (dharma) and the 10th house (karma) are associated.
basics.results: The native is sincere, devoted, righteous, and fortunate.

basics.definition: Lords of dusthanas (6th, 8th, 12th) occupy other dusthanas or conjoin other dusthana lords.
basics.results: The native experiences tremendous success, typically after an initial struggle.

magnitude_assessment.rule: The yoga-forming planets should be free from afflictions from functional malefics.
The conjunction or aspect forming the yoga should be close (e.g., within 6 degrees) for full results.
The yoga-forming planets should not be combust, debilitated, or in an inimical house.
The strength of the yoga is magnified if the participating planets are in high amsas in the Dasa Varga scheme.

amsa_results.Gopuraamsa (4 good placements): Becomes a great king respected by many other kings.
amsa_results.Simhaasanaamsa (5 good placements): Becomes a great emperor who rules the whole world.

advanced_raja_yogas.definition: Chara Putra Karaka (PK) and Atma Karaka (AK) are conjoined, AND the lords of the 1st and 5th houses conjoin.
Lords of the 1st and 5th exchange signs (parivartana), AND AK and PK are in the 1st or 5th house, AND these planets are well-dignified or aspected by benefics.
The 9th lord and AK are in the 1st, 5th, or 7th house, aspected by benefics.
The 2nd, 4th, and 5th houses from both the lagna lord and AK are occupied by benefics.
The 3rd and 6th houses from both the lagna lord and AK are occupied or aspected by malefics.
The same planet is associated (owns, joins, or aspects) with Lagna, Hora Lagna (HL), and Ghati Lagna (GL).
Arudha Lagna (AL) and Darapada (A7) are not in mutual 2nd/12th or 6th/8th positions, which makes other Raja Yogas in the chart more effective.

dhana_yogas_wealth.basic_principle: The 5th and 9th lords, and planets joining them, are capable of giving money. The 2nd and 11th houses and their lords should also be strong. A benefic exalted in the 2nd house makes one very rich.

lagna_specific_yogas.combination_1: Sun is in the 5th, and Saturn, Moon, and Jupiter are in the 11th.
lagna_specific_yogas.combination_2: Mars is in lagna, conjoined or aspected by Mercury, Venus, and Saturn.

lagna_specific_yogas.combination_1: Mercury is in the 5th, and Moon, Mars, and Jupiter are in the 11th.
lagna_specific_yogas.combination_2: Venus is in lagna, conjoined or aspected by Mercury and Saturn.

daridra_yogas_poverty.general_principles: Dusthanas (6th, 8th, 12th) and their lords are detrimental to wealth. Affliction to lagna, lagna lord, and trine lords by them can cause poverty. Affliction by maraka planets (lords/occupants of 2nd and 7th) worsens the situation. Association with trine lords is a saving factor. The 2nd house and its lord's strength are crucial.

specific_yogas.definition: The 1st and 12th lords exchange signs and are associated with a maraka planet.
The 1st and 6th lords exchange signs and are associated with a maraka planet.
Lagna or Moon is with Ketu, AND the lagna lord is in the 8th house, AND the lagna lord is associated with a maraka planet.
The 5th lord is in the 6th and the 9th lord is in the 12th, with aspects from maraka planets.

introduction_and_purpose.rule: Ashtakavarga is a system for analyzing a chart from a group of 8 reference points (the 7 planets and the Lagna).

introduction_and_purpose.purpose: It provides a simplified method to judge the strength of planets and houses, especially for analyzing transits, by quantifying whether a planet's position is favorable from multiple viewpoints simultaneously.

benefic_malefic_points.rule: For each of the 7 planets (Sun to Saturn), there is a predefined set of houses where its transit is considered benefic (favorable) as seen from each of the 8 reference points (Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, and Lagna).

terminology.benefic_point: Called a 'rekha' (line) or 'sthana', represented by the number 1.
terminology.malefic_point: Called a 'bindu' (dot) or 'karana', represented by the number 0.

benefic_malefic_points.note: The book follows Parasara's convention. Some other schools of astrology reverse this terminology.

bhinna_ashtakavarga_bav.definition: Bhinna Ashtakavarga (BAV) is a chart prepared for each of the 7 planets individually.
bhinna_ashtakavarga_bav.calculation_rule: For each planet's BAV chart, the number in a rasi is the total count of benefic points (rekhas) that planet receives in that rasi from all 8 reference points. The count in each rasi will range from 0 to 8.

samudaaya_ashtakavarga_sav.definition: Samudaaya or Sarva Ashtakavarga (SAV) is a consolidated chart showing the overall strength of each rasi.
samudaaya_ashtakavarga_sav.calculation_rule: For each rasi, sum the BAV rekhas of all seven planets (Sun to Saturn) in that rasi. The result is the SAV score for that rasi.

prastaara_ashtakavarga_pav.definition: Prastaara Ashtakavarga (PAV) is a 'spread-out' table or chart for a single planet.
prastaara_ashtakavarga_pav.purpose: It shows exactly which of the 8 references contribute a benefic point (rekha) to that planet in each of the 12 rasis. It is used for more detailed transit analysis.

bav_interpretation.rule: When analyzing a planet's BAV, the number of rekhas in a rasi indicates its strength and favorability for transit or natal placement in that rasi.

sav_interpretation.rule: The SAV score of a rasi indicates the overall strength of that house and its favorability for transits.

scoring.30 or more rekhas: Strong and favorable. Matters of this house flourish.
scoring.Less than 25 rekhas: Weak and unfavorable. Matters of this house suffer.

application_in_divisional_charts.rule: Ashtakavarga principles and calculations are applicable to all divisional charts, not just the rasi chart.
application_in_divisional_charts.prediction_rule: To analyze a specific area of life (e.g., career with D-10), calculate the SAV for that divisional chart. The strength of the houses (lagna, 10th, etc.) within that divisional chart's SAV provides crucial insights.

sodhya_pinda_calculations.purpose: Used for predicting the timing of key events, particularly in transit analysis.

step_1_trikona_sodhana.name: Trikona Sodhana (Trinal Reduction)
step_1_trikona_sodhana.description: This reduction is applied to a planet's BAV, working on each set of trines (Ar/Le/Sg, Ta/Vi/Cp, Ge/Li/Aq, Cn/Sc/Pi) separately.
rules: If at least one rasi in the trine has 0 rekhas, no reduction is done for that trine.
If all three rasis have the same number of rekhas, reduce all three values to 0.
Otherwise, find the lowest value among the three, and subtract this lowest value from all three rasis.

step_2_ekaadhipatya_sodhana.name: Ekaadhipatya Sodhana (Co-owned Reduction)
step_2_ekaadhipatya_sodhana.description: This reduction is applied to the results of the Trikona Sodhana, working on pairs of signs owned by the same planet (e.g., Ar/Sc, Ta/Li, etc.). The result is the Sodhita Ashtakavarga (SoAV).
rules: If at least one of the two rasis has a value of 0, no reduction is done.
If both rasis are occupied by planets, no reduction is done.
If one rasi is occupied and the other is empty: If the empty rasi has a lower value, it becomes 0. If the empty rasi has a higher value, its value is replaced by the value of the occupied rasi.
If both rasis are empty: If they have the same value, both become 0. If they have different values, the higher value is replaced by the lower value.

step_3_pinda_calculation.name: Rasi Pinda
step_3_pinda_calculation.calculation: Using the final SoAV chart, multiply the value in each rasi by that rasi's fixed multiplier (as per Table 28). Sum the 12 products to get the Rasi Pinda.

step_3_pinda_calculation.name: Graha Pinda
step_3_pinda_calculation.calculation: Using the SoAV chart, multiply the value in the rasi occupied by each of the 7 natal planets by that planet's fixed multiplier (as per Table 29). Sum the 7 products to get the Graha Pinda.

step_3_pinda_calculation.name: Sodhya Pinda
step_3_pinda_calculation.calculation: Sodhya Pinda = Rasi Pinda + Graha Pinda. This is calculated for each planet.

functional_nature_of_planets.rule: Planets have a functional nature (benefic, malefic, neutral) based on the houses they rule for a specific lagna, which is distinct from their natural (naisargika) nature.

house_lordship_rules.functional_nature: A natural benefic ruling a quadrant becomes a functional malefic. A natural malefic ruling a quadrant becomes functionally neutral.
Functionally Neutral (with the 8th being the most malefic of the three).

functional_nature_of_planets.yoga_karaka: A planet owning both a quadrant and a trine becomes a 'Yogakaraka' (an excellent, highly favorable planet).

placement_rules.favorable_placement: Placement in quadrants and trines brings good results.
Placement in the 3rd, 6th, 8th, or 12th houses brings good results by spoiling the negative significations of those houses.

baadhakas_troublemakers.definition: For a house, the 'baadhaka sthaana' (troubling spot) is a specific house that can cause obstructions. Its lord is the 'baadhaka' (troublemaker).

baadhakas_troublemakers.calculation_rule: The baadhaka sthaana is determined by the mobility of the rasi containing the house in question:

rules_by_mobility.Movable Rasi: The 11th house from it is the baadhaka sthaana.
rules_by_mobility.Fixed Rasi: The 9th house from it is the baadhaka sthaana.
rules_by_mobility.Dual Rasi: The 7th house from it is the baadhaka sthaana.

baadhakas_troublemakers.prediction_rule: The periods of the baadhaka planet or planets occupying the baadhaka sthaana can create obstructions and troubles related to the matters of the original house.

chart_interpretation_guidelines.action: Choose the correct Divisional Chart for the matter of interest (e.g., D-16 for vehicles, D-9 for marriage, D-10 for career).
Within that divisional chart, choose the correct House that represents the specific question (e.g., in D-24 for learning, the 4th house for formal education, the 5th for intelligence).
Choose the correct Reference point for counting houses. Use Lagna for 'true self' matters, Arudha Lagna (AL) for 'perceived self' and status matters, or relevant karakas if they are strong.

Determine if a House or its corresponding Arudha Pada is more appropriate. Houses relate to the reality of a matter, while Arudha Padas relate to the 'maya' or perception of it (e.g., A4 in D-24 for the school one attends).

Analyze all Influences on the chosen house/arudha, including planetary aspects (graha drishti), sign aspects (rasi drishti), argalas (interventions), and planets in surrounding houses (trines, quadrants, upachayas, dusthanas).
Apply Standard Results from classics, considering planetary strengths, avasthas, ashtakavarga, and the presence of specific yogas.

analysis_of_family_members.rule: Analyze the fortunes of specific family members using the relevant divisional chart.

analysis_of_family_members.method: To analyze a relative, treat the rasi containing the lord of the house representing that relative as a temporary lagna for them.

analysis_of_family_members.siblings_d3: In D-3, the 3rd house shows the first younger sibling, the 5th shows the second younger, etc. The 11th shows the first elder, the 9th shows the second elder, etc. Counting is forward from odd lagnas and backward from even lagnas.

analysis_of_family_members.children_d7: In D-7, the 5th lord's position indicates the first child, the 7th lord's position the second child, the 9th lord's position the third, and so on. Counting is forward from odd lagnas and backward from even lagnas.

special_yogas_mentioned.definition: Any two of Saturn, Ketu, and Venus are conjoined, and the third aspects them. The yoga is more powerful if it involves the 8th house and if the Atma Karaka (AK) is also involved.
special_yogas_mentioned.results: The native becomes a 'tapaswi' - one who pursues a goal with single-minded dedication, often related to research, spirituality, or uncovering secrets.

introduction_and_ethics.rule: An estimation of longevity is useful in matchmaking and for cautioning clients, but an ethical astrologer should not scare a client by predicting death.
The Rasi chart (D-1) is of utmost importance in seeing death, as death relates to physical existence. Rudramsa (D-11) and Trimsamsa (D-30) can also provide insights.

marakas_killers.rule: The 3rd house (vitality) and the 8th house (longevity) are the houses of life. Their strength and the strength of their lords contribute to good longevity.
The 2nd and 7th houses are the houses of death (maraka sthanas), being the 12th from the 3rd and 8th houses respectively.
The lords of the 2nd and 7th houses are primary maraka grahas.
A malefic planet that powerfully conjoins or aspects (with graha drishti) the 2nd or 7th houses, or their lords, also qualifies as a maraka graha.

marakas_killers.prediction_rule: In dasa systems used for timing death, the periods of maraka sthanas (for rasi dasas) and maraka grahas (for planet dasas) can bring death.

steps: Find the lord of the 8th house from the Lagna, using the special counting method from Table 32.
Find the lord of the 8th house from the 7th house, also using Table 32.
The stronger of these two planets becomes Rudra (significator of suffering and destruction).
If the weaker planet is severely afflicted (debilitated, in an inimical sign, conjoined/aspected by malefics), it becomes Rudra instead.

rudra_trishoola_maheswara.rule: The three trines (1st, 5th, 9th) from the rasi occupied by Rudra are called the Trishoola rasis. One of these rasis can bring death during its Shoola dasa.

steps: The default Maheswara (significator of soul's liberation) is the lord of the 8th house from the Atma Karaka (AK).
Exception 1: If this 8th lord is in its own or exaltation rasi, then Maheswara is the stronger of the 8th and 12th lords counted from that planet's position.
Exception 2: If Rahu or Ketu joins AK or is in the 8th house from AK, find the 6th lord from AK instead of the 8th lord.
Exception 3: If Rahu becomes Maheswara, use Mercury instead. If Ketu becomes Maheswara, use Jupiter instead.

pairs_to_analyze: Pair 1: Lagna Lord and the 8th Lord (calculated using Table 32).
analysis_steps: For each pair, determine if the two components are in Movable, Fixed, or Dual rasis.
Use Table 33 to find the longevity category (Short, Middle, Long) for each pair based on the combination of their sign mobilities.

Combine the results: If two or three pairs indicate the same category, that category dominates. If all three are different, the result from Pair 3 (Lagna/HL) is preferred, unless Moon is in the 1st or 7th house, in which case Pair 2 (Moon/Saturn) is preferred.
Determine which is stronger: the Lagna or the 7th house.
Take the stronger of the two as the reference point.
Find the lord of the 8th house from this reference point.
Determine the placement of this 8th lord relative to the reference point:

analysis_steps.placement: In a quadrant (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th) from the reference
In a panaphara (2nd, 5th, 8th, 11th) from the reference
In an apoklima (3rd, 6th, 9th, 12th) from the reference

purpose_of_different_strengths.purpose: Determines which planet is the 'leader' in a yoga or when multiple planets influence a house. The planet with the highest shadbala is most likely to deliver the results on behalf of the group.
Shows a planet's ability to deliver its own results in harmony with other planetary forces in the chart. A high score indicates support from other planets.
Represents the state of a planet (e.g., age, alertness, mood), which modifies its ability to give results. A planet must be in a good state to give good results.
Shows the overall strength and effectiveness of a planet across one's entire life, rather than in a specific area. It indicates the planet's ability to play an important role.

age_related_avasthas.rule: The effectiveness of a planet is modified by its 'age', determined by its degree within a rasi. The results are given as a fraction of the planet's full potential.

alertness_related_avasthas.rule: A planet's alertness level, determined by its dignity, affects the magnitude of its results.

states.condition: In its own or exaltation rasi.
In a rasi owned by a friendly or neutral planet.
In its debilitation or an enemy's rasi.

attitude_and_mood_avasthas.rule: Planets give results according to their mood or attitude, which is determined by their placement and associations.

states.condition: In the 5th house, joined by Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, or Ketu.
In its exaltation or moolatrikona rasi.
In an enemy's rasi, or conjoined/aspected by enemies, or conjoined by Saturn.
In a watery rasi, aspected by enemies, with no benefic aspect.
Conjoined by the Sun and aspected by malefics or enemies.

activity_related_sayanaadi_avasthas.formula: Avastha Index = [ (C * P * A) + M + G + L ] % 12 (remainder after dividing by 12)

variables.C: Constellation number occupied by the planet (1 for Aswini, 2 for Bharani, etc.).
variables.P: Planet's index (Sun=1, Moon=2, Mars=3, Mercury=4, Jupiter=5, Venus=6, Saturn=7).
variables.A: Navamsa number within the rasi (1 to 9).
variables.M: Constellation number occupied by the Moon.
variables.G: Ghati running at birth (time from sunrise in minutes / 24).
variables.L: Rasi number of the Lagna (Aries=1, etc.).

activity_related_sayanaadi_avasthas.strength_calculation: Strength Index = [ (Avastha Index^2) + Name Sound Number + Planetary Adjustment ] % 3
special_prediction_rules: A malefic in the 7th house in Nidraa avastha is auspicious.
A malefic in the 5th house in Sayana or Nidraa avastha is auspicious.
A malefic in Bhojana avastha destroys the house it occupies.
A benefic or a planet in own/exaltation rasi, occupying the 1st, 5th, 7th, or 10th house in Prakaasana, Nrityalipsaa, or Kautuka avasthas, brings Raja Yoga.

stronger_co_lord.purpose: To determine the primary lord of Scorpio (Mars vs. Ketu) and Aquarius (Saturn vs. Rahu) for calculating arudha padas and some rasi dasas.
stronger_co_lord.basic_rule: If one of the co-lords is in the rasi itself, the other one is taken as the primary lord.
hierarchical_rules: 1. The planet joined by more planets is stronger.
2. The planet conjoined or aspected (rasi drishti) by more of the following is stronger: Jupiter, Mercury, its dispositor.
3. An exalted planet is stronger than a non-exalted one.
4. A planet in a Dual rasi is stronger than one in a Fixed rasi, which is stronger than one in a Movable rasi.
5. If a tie persists, the planet that is more advanced in its rasi is stronger (for Rahu/Ketu, measure from the end of the rasi).

stronger_rasi.purpose: To compare the strength between two rasis, typically for determining the starting point of rasi dasas like Narayana dasa or for finding the stronger owned sign for graha arudhas.
hierarchical_rules: 1. The rasi containing more planets is stronger.
2. The rasi occupied or aspected (rasi drishti) by more of the following is stronger: Jupiter, Mercury, the rasi's lord.
3. A rasi containing an exalted planet is stronger than one that does not.
4. A rasi whose lord is in a rasi of a different oddity (odd vs. even) is stronger.
5. A Dual rasi is stronger than a Fixed rasi, which is stronger than a Movable rasi.
6. If a tie persists, the rasi whose lord has a higher advancement (longitude) within its sign is stronger.

vimsottari_dasa_introduction.rule: Vimsottari dasa is considered the most suitable dasa system for the current era (Kali Yuga).
It is a 120-year cycle, corresponding to the theoretical maximum lifespan (paramaayush) in Kali Yuga.

vimsottari_dasa_introduction.hierarchy: The dasa is structured in levels: Mahadasa (MD), Antardasa (AD), Pratyantardasa (PD), Sookshma dasa (SD), Prana-antardasa (PAD), and Deha-antardasa (DAD).

steps: 1. Find the nakshatra (constellation) occupied by the Moon at birth.
2. Calculate the fraction of the nakshatra that is yet to be traversed by the Moon (Remaining longitude / 13°20').
3. The first Mahadasa (MD) at birth belongs to the lord of the Moon's nakshatra.
4. The remaining duration of this first MD is calculated by multiplying its total length (from Table 38) by the fraction found in step 2.
5. Subsequent Mahadasas follow the fixed sequence and have their full duration as per Table 38.

vimsottari_dasa_computation.note: The sequence in the book starts with Sun, but the universally accepted sequence starts with Ketu as per the nakshatra lords. The book's table is ordered differently, but the sequence is cyclical (e.g., after Venus comes Sun).

vimsottari_dasa_computation.rule: Each Mahadasa is divided into 9 Antardasas. The sequence of Antardasa lords starts with the Mahadasa lord and follows the standard Vimsottari sequence. The duration of each Antardasa is proportional to its lord's Mahadasa length. (AD length = (MD length of AD lord / 120) * Total MD length).
For the first MD at birth, the antardasas are calculated based on the full duration of that MD, not the remaining portion. This means some antardasas may have already passed before birth.
The author uses 'savana years' of 360 days for calculating nakshatra dasa durations, though the use of solar years (365.2425 days) is also common.

vimsottari_dasa_variations.rule: The starting dasa can be reckoned from the lords of nakshatras other than the birth nakshatra, while the balance of the first dasa is still calculated based on the Moon's position in its birth nakshatra.
Start the dasa sequence from the lord of the 4th nakshatra from the Moon's nakshatra. Used mainly for longevity determination.

Start the dasa sequence from the lord of the 5th nakshatra from the Moon's nakshatra. May be preferred for general results (phalita) if the sign containing the 5th star is stronger than the Moon's sign.
Start the dasa sequence from the lord of the 8th nakshatra from the Moon's nakshatra. Used mainly for longevity determination.
The entire Vimsottari Dasa calculation can be based on the longitude of the Lagna instead of the Moon, especially if the Lagna is significantly more powerful than the Moon.

interpretation_rules.rule: During its dasa or antardasa, a planet gives the results indicated by its positions (house, sign, aspects, yogas) in the various divisional charts relevant to the matter in question.
The level of the dasa period determines the primary reference point for analysis:
A planet involved in a specific yoga tends to give the results of that yoga during its period, according to the 'Tripod of Life' principle:
For analyzing an antardasa, the position of the Mahadasa lord can be treated as a temporary lagna.

application_as_ayur_dasa_longevity.rule: Vimsottari Dasa can be used to time death.
application_as_ayur_dasa_longevity.prediction_rule: Death can occur during the dasa/antardasa of maraka planets (lords of the 2nd and 7th houses) or other strong killers.

application_as_ayur_dasa_longevity.rule: For longevity analysis, the appropriate variation (from the 1st, 4th, or 8th star) should be chosen. The correct starting star is the one located in the strongest sign among the three, where strength is determined by the influence of marakas and malefics.

limitations_and_cautions.rule: If all quadrants from the stronger of Lagna and Moon are occupied, other dasa systems like 'Kendradi Graha Dasa' may be more appropriate.

Birth time errors significantly impact dasa calculations. An error of 'm' minutes in birth time can cause an approximate error of (m * n / 4) days in dasa timings, where 'n' is the year-length of the first dasa lord. This makes sub-periods below Pratyantardasa unreliable without a rectified birth time.

introduction_and_purpose.rule: Ashtottari dasa is a 108-year cycle system, which corresponds to the concept of a 'full life' (poornaayush).

purpose_debate: Some scholars suggest it is best used as an 'ayur dasa' for determining longevity.
Others use it as a 'phalita dasa' for general predictions.
Because only 8 karakas (7 planets + Rahu) are used, it may specifically show events related to sustenance, achievements, raja yogas, and moksha.

applicability_conditions.rule: The conditions for applying Ashtottari dasa are highly controversial, with several differing views.

applicability_conditions.view_1: It is applicable in all charts.

applicability_conditions.view_2: It is applicable if Rahu is in a quadrant or a trine from the lagna lord, provided Rahu is not in the lagna itself.

applicability_conditions.view_3: It is applicable for daytime births during Krishna Paksha (waning moon) and for nighttime births during Sukla Paksha (waxing moon).

steps: 1. The zodiac is divided into 8 arcs, each ruled by a planet and covering a specific group of nakshatras (as defined in Table 39).
2. Locate the longitude of the natal Moon to identify which of the 8 arcs it falls into.
3. The planet ruling that arc will be the lord of the first dasa at birth.
4. Calculate the fraction of the arc that is yet to be traversed by the Moon (Remaining Arc Length / Total Arc Length).
5. The remaining duration of the first dasa at birth is this fraction multiplied by the full dasa length of the ruling planet.

dasa_calculation.note: The sequence is cyclical. After Venus, it returns to Sun.

antardasa_calculation.rule: The sequence of antardasas within a mahadasa starts with the planet that comes *after* the mahadasa lord in the Ashtottari sequence. The mahadasa lord's antardasa comes last.
The total length of a mahadasa is divided among the eight antardasas proportionally, based on their respective mahadasa lengths. (AD Length = (Full MD Length of AD Lord / 108) * Current MD Length).

introduction_and_core_concept.rule: Narayana dasa is a versatile Rasi Dasa used for general predictions (Phalita dasa).

introduction_and_core_concept.concept: It is based on the principle of a 'progressed lagna'. In each dasa period, a new rasi acts as a temporary lagna (or a temporary 7th house) from which results are judged.

introduction_and_core_concept.application_rule: Narayana dasa can be computed differently for each divisional chart to predict events specific to the matters of that chart.

dasa_progression_sequence.rule: The dasa sequence starts from the stronger of the Lagna and the 7th house. This stronger rasi is called the 'dasa seed'.

dasa_progression_sequence.rule_name: Step 2: Determine Progression Type based on Dasa Seed's Mobility

types.progression_type: Regular (Brahma): Dasas proceed sequentially through the zodiac (1st, 2nd, 3rd, etc.).
Every 6th (Shiva): Dasas jump to the 6th sign from the previous dasa sign.
Trinal (Vishnu): Dasas cover the trines from the dasa seed, then the trines from its 10th, then from its 7th, then from its 4th.

dasa_progression_sequence.rule: The direction is determined by the 9th house from the 'dasa seed'. If the 9th house is in an odd-footed sign (Ar, Ta, Ge, Li, Sc, Sg), the direction is forward (zodiacal). If it is in an even-footed sign (Cn, Le, Vi, Cp, Aq, Pi), the direction is backward (anti-zodiacal).

dasa_progression_sequence.rule_name: Step 4: Apply Exceptions to Dasa Seed

exceptions.effect: The progression becomes regular (Brahma type) and zodiacal (forward), overriding all other progression and direction rules.
The calculated direction of progression is reversed (forward becomes backward, backward becomes forward).

dasa_length.rule: Dasa Length (in years) = (Count from Dasa Rasi to its Lord) - 1.
The count from the dasa rasi to its lord is forward if the dasa rasi is odd-footed, and backward if the dasa rasi is even-footed.

exceptions.condition: The lord is in the dasa rasi itself (count is 1).
The lord of the dasa rasi is exalted.
exceptions.effect: Add one year to the calculated dasa length.

exceptions.condition: The lord of the dasa rasi is debilitated.
exceptions.effect: Subtract one year from the calculated dasa length.

dasa_length.rule: For Scorpio or Aquarius dasas, the stronger of the two co-lords must be used to determine the dasa length.
In the second cycle of dasas, the length of a rasi's dasa is calculated as: 12 - (Dasa length in the first cycle).

antardasas_sub_periods.rule: Each dasa is divided into 12 equal antardasas. If a dasa is 'n' years long, each antardasa is 'n' months long.
The antardasa sequence begins from the rasi containing the lord of the 'antardasa seed'. The 'antardasa seed' is the stronger of the dasa rasi and the 7th rasi from it.
The sequence proceeds sequentially (1st, 2nd, 3rd, etc.) from the starting rasi. The direction is forward if the starting rasi is an odd sign, and backward if it is an even sign.

exceptions.effect: The antardasa sequence becomes forward, regardless of the starting rasi's odd/even nature.
The normal direction of the antardasa sequence is reversed.

interpretation.rule: During a dasa, the progressed lagna, called 'dasa lagna', is used as the primary reference for analysis. If dasas started from the natal Lagna, the dasa rasi is the dasa lagna. If dasas started from the natal 7th house, the 7th from the dasa rasi is the dasa lagna.
The rasi containing the lord of the 'dasa lagna' is called the 'paaka rasi'.

interpretation.rule_name: General House Placements from Dasa Lagna

placements.result: Good name, fame, favors from authorities.

interpretation.rule_name: Strength of Lords from Dasa Lagna
interpretation.rule: If the lord of the dasa lagna, or the lord of a trine or quadrant from it, is exalted or in its own house, it gives excellent results. Debilitation of these lords is bad. However, if the lord of a dusthana (6, 8, 12) from the dasa lagna is debilitated, it gives good results.
The dasa of a rasi containing an important natal point will activate its results. For example:
examples: Dasa of the rasi containing Raajya Pada (A10) can give success in career.
Dasa of the rasi containing Upapada (UL) can bring marriage.
Dasa of the rasi containing GL can bring power.

interpretation.rule: The results of an antardasa are judged by the house occupied by the antardasa lord from the dasa rasi. Also, antardasa rasis that aspect key Arudha Padas (like GL, UL, A3) can trigger events related to them.

A dasa can be divided into three equal parts: the first part is dominated by the nature of the dasa rasi itself, the second part by its lord, and the third part by the occupants and aspecting planets.

narayana_dasa_of_vargas.rule: Narayana Dasa can be calculated for any divisional chart (varga) to time events specific to that chart's domain (e.g., D-10 for career, D-9 for marriage).
The 'seed' for D-n is the nth house. If 'n' is greater than 12, subtract multiples of 12 until the number is between 1 and 12. (e.g., D-16 seed is the 4th house; D-27 seed is the 3rd house).

steps: 1. Determine the seed house for the divisional chart in question (e.g., 10th house for D-10).
2. Find the lord of that seed house in the RASI chart.
3. Locate the position of this lord in the DIVISIONAL chart.
4. Treat this rasi (from step 3) as the temporary lagna and calculate the entire Narayana Dasa (progression, length, etc.) using the rules as if it were a rasi chart.

narayana_dasa_of_vargas.warning: When interpreting Narayana Dasa of a varga, the concept of 'dasa lagna' as the progressed lagna does not apply. The interpretation is based on the dasa of rasis containing significant points within that varga.

sub_divisions_and_timing.rule: The hierarchy of sub-periods (Pratyantardasa, Sookshma, etc.) is calculated by dividing the parent period into 12 equal parts.

sub_divisions_and_timing.timing_principle: The measurement of time and the division of periods in rasi dasas like Narayana Dasa are based on the motion of the Sun. A 'dasa year' is 360 degrees of solar motion, a 'dasa month' is 30 degrees, and a 'dasa day' is 1 degree. This ensures precise timing.

introduction_and_purpose.rule: Lagna Kendradi Rasi Dasa is a phalita dasa (for general results) that shows material success well.

introduction_and_purpose.principle: The dasa sequence is based on a progression ruled by Sri Maha Lakshmi (goddess of wealth), covering quadrants first, then panapharas, and finally apoklimas.

introduction_and_purpose.comparison: This dasa is similar to Sudasa, but Sudasa starts from Sree Lagna and is considered superior for predicting material success.

dasa_sequence.action: Determine the 'dasa seed': the stronger of the Lagna and the 7th house.

rules: The direction is forward (zodiacal) if the Lagna is in an odd sign.
The direction is backward (anti-zodiacal) if the Lagna is in an even sign.

dasa_sequence.action: Apply direction exceptions based on the 'dasa seed' rasi:

exceptions.condition: Saturn is in the 'dasa seed' rasi.
exceptions.effect: The dasa order becomes forward, overriding the Lagna's odd/even sign rule.

exceptions.condition: Ketu is in the 'dasa seed' rasi.
exceptions.effect: The normal direction of progression is reversed (forward becomes backward, backward becomes forward).

dasa_sequence.action: Establish the dasa order based on the 'Kendraadi gati' (quadrants-first movement):
order: First 4 dasas: The quadrants (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th) from the 'dasa seed'.
Next 4 dasas: The panapharas (2nd, 5th, 8th, 11th) from the 'dasa seed'.
Last 4 dasas: The apoklimas (3rd, 6th, 9th, 12th) from the 'dasa seed'.

dasa_length.rule: The rules for calculating the duration (length in years) of each rasi dasa are exactly the same as those used for Narayana Dasa.

interpretation.principle: As this dasa is ruled by Lakshmi (goddess of prosperity), it is primarily used to identify periods of material prosperity.

interpretation.prediction_rule: Success can be indicated during the dasa of a rasi that contains significant points related to power and status, such as:
indicators: A rasi having a strong, unobstructed argala from the Amatya Karaka (AmK).

clarification_and_controversy.rule: This dasa system is based on 'kendraadi gati' (quadrants-first movement) and should not be confused with 'Mandooka dasa', which is based on 'mandooki gati' (3rd/11th jumps).

introduction_and_purpose.rule: Sudasa, also known as 'Sree Lagna Kendradi Rasi Dasa', is a rasi dasa used to predict materialistic matters such as money, power, and authority.

introduction_and_purpose.application_rule: Favorable periods for financial prosperity, career success, and rise in status are identified using Sudasa.

computation.action: The dasa sequence starts from the rasi containing Sree Lagna (SL).
Determine the direction of progression: forward (zodiacal) if SL is in an odd sign, and backward (anti-zodiacal) if SL is in an even sign.
Establish the dasa order based on the 'Kendraadi gati' (quadrants-first movement) from SL:
order: First 4 dasas: The quadrants (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th) from SL.
Next 4 dasas: The panapharas (2nd, 5th, 8th, 11th) from SL.
Last 4 dasas: The apoklimas (3rd, 6th, 9th, 12th) from SL.

computation.action: Calculate the duration (length in years) of each rasi dasa using the same rules as Narayana Dasa.
Apply the exception for the first dasa: Only a fraction of the first dasa is remaining at birth.
computation.formula: Remaining Fraction = (30° – SL’s longitude within its sign) / 30°.

interpretation.prediction_rule: Dasas of rasis associated with Hora Lagna (HL) bring financial prosperity. Association means the dasa rasi contains HL, is the 7th from HL, is aspected by HL, or its lord occupies/aspects HL.
Dasas of rasis associated with Ghati Lagna (GL) bring power and authority. The rules of association are the same as for HL.
Dasas of upachaya houses (3rd, 6th, 10th, 11th) from Arudha Lagna (AL) bring growth of status. The dasa of the 11th from AL is particularly favorable.
Dasas of the 8th and 12th houses from Arudha Lagna (AL) can bring setbacks to one's status.

introduction_and_purpose.rule: Niryaana Shoola Dasa is presented as one of the most reliable rasi dasa systems specifically for the purpose of timing death (Ayur Dasa).

introduction_and_purpose.note: The author distinguishes this from another dasa also called 'Shoola Dasa' to avoid confusion, hence the name 'Niryaana Shoola Dasa'.

dasa_sequence_calculation.rule: The dasa sequence begins from the stronger of the 2nd house and the 8th house.
The progression of dasas is forward (zodiacal) if the starting rasi is an odd sign. The progression is backward (anti-zodiacal) if the starting rasi is an even sign.

dasa_length_calculation.rule: The duration of each dasa is fixed based on the mobility of the dasa rasi.

antardasa_calculation.rule: The calculation method for antardasas is the same as that used for Narayana Dasa.

interpretation_for_timing_death.rule_name: Primary Rule for Dasa of Death
interpretation_for_timing_death.rule: Death most commonly occurs during the dasa of one of the three Trishoola rasis.
interpretation_for_timing_death.application_note: The specific Trishoola rasi that can cause death is selected based on the native's pre-determined longevity category (e.g., a Trishoola rasi whose dasa falls within the 'middle life' span for a native with middle life longevity).

interpretation_for_timing_death.rule_name: Secondary Rules for Dasa of Death
rules: The dasa of a strong maraka rasi can bring death.
The dasa of a rasi containing strong maraka grahas can bring death.
If Trishoola rasis do not bring death, the dasa of the rasi that is 12th from the rasi occupied by Rudra can bring death.

prediction_rules.rule: The antardasa can be the 6th, 7th, 8th, or 12th rasi, counted from the mahadasa rasi.
The antardasa can be a rasi that aspects the navamsa sign of the 8th lord (where the 8th house is counted from the mahadasa rasi).
If a rasi satisfies both Possibility 1 and Possibility 2, it becomes a strong candidate for being the antardasa of death.

introduction_and_purpose.rule: Shoola Dasa is a rasi dasa used for timing death, diseases, suffering, and the death of relatives.

introduction_and_purpose.distinction: This dasa is different from 'Niryaana Shoola Dasa'. Shoola Dasa represents the motion of Shiva's force, which is constant and fixed, while Niryaana Shoola Dasa represents the variable motion of 'praana' (life force).

steps: 1. Determine the stronger of the Lagna and the 7th house.
2. The dasa sequence starts from this stronger rasi.
3. The progression of dasas is always in the regular zodiacal (forward) order from the starting rasi.

computation.rule: Each mahadasa has a fixed duration of 9 years.

Each mahadasa is divided into 12 equal antardasas, each lasting 9 months. The antardasa sequence is found by treating the mahadasa rasi as a temporary lagna and applying the same rules used for calculating the main dasa sequence.

interpretation_for_timing_death.core_principle: Death occurs when Shiva's motion (represented by the Shoola Dasa rasi) 'hits' or aligns with key points related to the native's perceived existence (maya).

interpretation_for_timing_death.rule_name: Primary Indicators for Dasa of Death
interpretation_for_timing_death.rule: Death is likely to occur in the dasa of a rasi that is a trine (1st, 5th, 9th) from the Arudha Lagna (AL).

interpretation_for_timing_death.rule_name: Secondary Indicators for Dasa of Death
interpretation_for_timing_death.rule: The dasas of the 3rd and 8th houses from the Arudha Lagna (AL) can also bring death, especially if they are occupied or aspected by malefics or marakas. The 8th house here should be counted using the special method from Table 32.

interpretation_for_timing_death.rule_name: Filtering Rule for Dasa of Death
interpretation_for_timing_death.rule: A rasi is unlikely to cause death if it is occupied or aspected by the Atma Karaka (AK) or Jupiter, unless that planet itself is Rudra.
The potential dasa of death must fall within the native's predetermined longevity category:

categories.Short Life (0-36 years): Death occurs in one of the first 4 Shoola dasas.
categories.Middle Life (36-72 years): Death occurs in one of the middle 4 Shoola dasas (5th, 6th, 7th, 8th).
categories.Long Life (72-108 years): Death occurs in one of the last 4 Shoola dasas (9th, 10th, 11th, 12th).

timing_death_of_relatives.rule: To time the death of a near relative, calculate a specific Shoola Dasa for that relative by treating the house representing them as a temporary lagna.

specific_shoola_dasas.starts_from: Stronger of the 7th and 1st houses (identical to native's own Shoola dasa).

timing_death_of_relatives.prediction_rule: The death of a relative can occur during the dasa of a rasi that is in a trine from the Sthira Karaka (fixed significator) of that relative.

timing_death_of_relatives.secondary_prediction_rule: The dasas of rasis that are trines from the Arudha Pada of the house representing the relative (e.g., A9 for father) can also give death.

introduction_and_purpose.rule: Kalachakra Dasa is described by Sage Parasara as 'the most respectable of all dasa systems'.

introduction_and_purpose.principle: It shows how the 'wheel of Time' (Kalachakra) unfolds events in an individual's life.

introduction_and_purpose.core_focus: The dasa's focus is on the state of the 'inner self' and one's sense of 'connectedness' to life events, as its calculation is based on the Moon's navamsa position.

comparative_focus.focus: The state of the mind's view of events.
The actual direction and happenings in one's life.
How the inner self relates to and feels connected with those happenings.

introduction_and_purpose.applicability: The author opines that Kalachakra Dasa is applicable to all people, as Parasara imposed no conditions on its use.

nakshatra_groups_and_sequences.rule: Nakshatras are divided into two primary groups: 'Savya' (zodiacal) and 'Apasavya' (anti-zodiacal).

Each group (Savya and Apasavya) has its own unique 24-rasi sequence, which is repeated. This sequence is formed by a 'main' zodiacal or anti-zodiacal sequence of 12 rasis, followed by a 'mirrored' sequence of 12 rasis based on planetary ownership.
Each nakshatra pada (quarter) is associated with a specific sequence of 9 dasa rasis, which is a segment of the larger 24-rasi cycle corresponding to its group (Savya or Apasavya).
The total duration of the nine dasas for a given nakshatra pada is called its 'Paramaayush', which can be 100, 85, 83, or 86 years.

dasa_calculation_procedure.action: Find the nakshatra pada occupied by the natal Moon and identify its corresponding 9-rasi sequence and Paramaayush from the tables (Table 44-47).
Calculate the fraction of the nakshatra pada that was already traversed by the Moon at birth. (Fraction = Longitude traversed in pada / 3°20').
Multiply this fraction by the Paramaayush for that pada. This gives the portion of the 9-dasa cycle that was completed before birth.
Based on the elapsed duration, determine which of the nine dasas is running at birth and calculate the remaining balance.
Subsequent dasas follow the order of the 9-rasi sequence. After the 9 dasas for one pada are complete, the sequence continues with the 9 dasas of the next nakshatra pada.

dasa_lengths.rule: The duration of a dasa is determined by the planet that owns the dasa rasi, as per Table 48.

antardasa_calculation.rule: In any given mahadasa, the antardasa sequence consists of 9 rasis, starting from the mahadasa rasi itself and proceeding along the established Kalachakra sequence.
The total length of the mahadasa is distributed among the 9 antardasas proportionally, based on the standard dasa lengths of the antardasa rasis' lords (from Table 48).

basic_interpretation.rule: The dasa of a rasi gives results based on the natural significations of that rasi (e.g., Pisces dasa for spiritual activities).
More importantly, the dasa of a rasi gives the results of the house it contains and any planets occupying it, in the context of the relevant divisional chart.
The dasa of a rasi can also give the results of its lord's placement in the chart.

Samudaaya Ashtakavarga (SAV) is a critical tool for interpretation. A dasa of a rasi with 30 or more rekhas in the SAV of a specific divisional chart will bring favorable results for the matters of that chart.

deha_and_jeeva_rasis.definition: Deha Rasi represents the body; Jeeva Rasi represents the life/spirit.
